zh.lifeSummary: 當William Thomas De Staunton於1400年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓時，他的父親Thomas De Staunton32歲，母親Margaret Maperly28歲。他於1430年與Elizabeth Merdeley在英國諾廷加姆爾史道頓結婚。他去世於1482年8月9日在英國英格蘭素福爾可圖登哈姆享年82歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Staunton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: McEvilly
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mhílidh ‘son of the knight’, from Gaelic míleadh ‘warrior, knight’. In County Mayo and County Cork, a family of English origin Gaelicized their name from Staunton to Mac an Mhílidh, apparently because of the latter's presumed similarity to the name of their ancestor, Milo de Stanton.

variants.name: Stanton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When William Thomas De Staunton was born in 1400, in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas De Staunton, was 32 and his mother, Margaret Maperly, was 28. He married Elizabeth Merdeley in 1430, in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England. He died on 9 August 1482, in Tuddenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Merdeley en 1430, à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 9 août 1482, à Tuddenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Thomas De Staunton(이)가 1400년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas De Staunton(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Maperly(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1430년 영국 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에서 Elizabeth Merdeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1482년 8월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 투데함에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에 묻혔다.

Он умер 9 августа 1482 г. в местности тудденам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
